fontNodes.name: Bebas Neue
qualityNotes: Weight range is narrow or single-style, so this font should not be expected to cover multiple hierarchy levels.
This font appears often enough to dominate the system and should be justified by a strict role, not convenience.

fontNodes.name: DM Sans
qualityNotes: This font appears often enough to dominate the system and should be justified by a strict role, not convenience.
This font is spanning too many rhetorical roles, which weakens hierarchy discipline.

issues.title: Too many condensed display sans fonts are competing for the same job
issues.summary: The audit found several tall, forceful sans faces occupying the same hero/headline territory. This is classic role duplication and will make the system feel random unless we retire most of them.

issues.title: Accent script territory is duplicated
issues.summary: Multiple script fonts are present, but script should be a rare accent role. Without strict limits, this becomes faux-luxury very quickly in motion.

issues.title: Hardcoded font choices are bypassing the typography intelligence layer
issues.summary: Several active runtime components still embed explicit font stacks instead of routing through a single governed font system. That makes consistency impossible even with better taste rules.

issues.title: A few fonts are carrying too many roles by repetition alone
issues.summary: The frequency pattern suggests convenience-driven reuse rather than deliberate hierarchy. That is the exact expression-over-structure trap we want to avoid.

issues.title: Legacy and active typography systems are drifting apart
issues.summary: The repo still contains a strong legacy preset layer alongside the newer editorial selector. Until they share one governed font graph, Prometheus will keep producing split-brain typography.

governanceGaps: neutral_sans exists, but it is not singular. DM Sans is still competing with Segoe UI and Arial Narrow in parts of the stack, so the missing piece is restriction, not discovery.
hero typography is overcrowded. The system needs 2-3 elite hero faces, not a broad shelf of equally expressive options.
accent usage is too open-ended. Script and decorative faces should be tightly quarantined to rare rhetorical moments.
